questions.question: If 18 January 2013 was Friday, then what was the day of the week on 19 December 2021?
questions.explanation: Odd days between18 January 2013 and 19 December 2021: The odd days in years → 8+2 [leap years 2016, 2020] =10 The odd days in months → 13+0+3+2+3+2+3+3+2+3+2+19=55 Total odd days =10+55=65, i.e. +2 days. So, the day on 19 December 2021 will be Friday +2 = Sunday

questions.question: Numbers1 to 6 are written on different faces of a dice. Three positions of this dice are shown in the figures below. Find the number on the face opposite to 6. ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997066e883a40.01650087.png) >एक पासे के अलग-अलग फलकों पर 1 से 6 तक की संख्याएँ लिखी हैं। नीचे दी गई आकृतियों में इस पासे की तीन स्थितियाँ दर्शाई गई हैं। 6 के विपरीत फलक पर संख्या ज्ञात कीजिए। ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997066e97fc65.84670756.png)
questions.explanation: From the first two dices, 4&5 are present on both, so, 2 and 3 are opposite faces. From the last two dices, 3&4 are present on both, so, 1 and 5 are opposite faces. Now, only pair of opposite faces is left that is 4 and 6. Hence, the correct answer is 4. > पहले दो पासों से, 4 और 5 दोनों पर मौजूद हैं, इसलिए, 2 और 3 विपरीत चेहरे हैं। अंतिम दो पासों से, 3 और 4 दोनों पर मौजूद हैं, इसलिए, 1 और 5 विपरीत चेहरे हैं। अब, विपरीत चेहरों की केवल जोड़ी बची है जो 4 और 6 है। इसलिए, सही उत्तर 4 है।

questions.question: Match the following: Viceroy/Governor Work A) Warren Hastings 1) Abolished used of Dastak B) Lord Rippon 2) Statistical Survey of India C) Lord Mayo 3) Factory Act 1881 D) Lord Lansdown 4) Factory Act 1891
questions.explanation: Warren Hastings [1773-85]- Aboilished Dastak system as it was misused by company employee and it was one of the major reasons for Battle of Buxar. Lord Mayo [1869-72]- Formed resolution of Financial Decentralisation, Established Statistical Survey of India. Lord Rippon [1880-84]- Repeal Vernacular Press Act, Called father of local Self government, To improve labour condition enacted first factory Act 1881. Lord Lansdowne [1888-94]- Set up Durand Commission to define Durand line between India-Afghanistan, Enacted Factory Act 1891.

Nino de Cunha [1529-38] - Transferred capital from Cochin to Goa in 1530. > फ्रांसिस्को डी अल्मेडा [1505-09] - ब्लू वाटर पॉलिसी/कार्टाज़ सिस्टम तैयार किया। अल्फांसो डी अल्बुकर्क [1509-15] - 1510 में गोवा पर कब्ज़ा किया और सती प्रथा को समाप्त किया। नीनो डी कुन्हा [1529-38] - 1530 में कोचीन से गोवा में राजधानी स्थानांतरित की।

questions.question: Which of the following organisations awards the Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences?
options: The Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences >रॉयल स्वीडिश एकेडमी ऑफ साइंसेस
questions.explanation: The Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences gives the Nobel Prize for Economics Sciences. The prize was established in 1968. In 2020 the prize was held by US economists 'Paul Milgrom and Robert B Willson. > रॉयल स्वीडिश एकेडमी ऑफ़ साइंसेस अर्थशास्त्र के लिए नोबेल पुरस्कार देती है। पुरस्कार की स्थापना 1968 में की गई थी। 2020 में यह पुरस्कार अमेरिकी अर्थशास्त्रियों 'पॉल मिलग्रोम और रॉबर्ट बी विल्सन' के पास था।

questions.question: Which article prohibits religious education in state-funded institutions?
questions.explanation: Right to Freedom of Religion:- 25- Freedom of conscience and free profession, practice and propagation of religion. 26- Freedom to manage religious affairs. 27- Freedom as to payment of taxes for promotion of any particular religion. 28 -Freedom as to attendance at religious instruction or religious worship in certain educational institutions. Cultural and Educational Rights:- 29 -Protection of interests of minorities. 30- Right of minorities to establish and administer educational institutions.

questions.question: Which amendment of constitution abolished Privy Purse? > संविधान के किस संशोधन द्वारा प्रिवी पर्स को समाप्त कर दिया गया?
questions.explanation: 21ˢᵗ Amendment in 1967 added Sindhi as official language. 24ᵗʰ Amendment in 1971mandated president to sign constitutional amendment bill. 40ᵗʰ Amendment in 1976 amended article 297 and specified limit of territorial water. > 1967 में 21वें संशोधन द्वारा सिंधी को आधिकारिक भाषा के रूप में शामिल किया गया। 1971 में 24वें संशोधन द्वारा राष्ट्रपति को संविधान संशोधन विधेयक पर हस्ताक्षर करने का आदेश दिया गया। 1976 में 40वें संशोधन द्वारा अनुच्छेद 297 में संशोधन किया गया तथा प्रादेशिक जल की सीमा निर्दिष्ट की गई।

questions.question: Identify the function of lipase in the process of digestion. > पाचन की प्रक्रिया में लिपेज़ का कार्य पहचानें।
options: Breaking down emulsified fat>इमल्सीफाइड वसा को तोड़ना
Secreting intestinal fluid>आंतों के तरल को स्रावित करना
questions.explanation: Lipase breaks down emulsified fat. Lipase and bile work together to digest and absorb fat in the body. > लिपेज़ इमल्सिफाइड वसा को तोड़ता है। लिपेज़ और पित्त शरीर में वसा को पचाने और अवशोषित करने के लिए मिलकर काम करते हैं।

questions.question: The division of the cytoplasm of a eukaryotic cell into two daughter cells is known as-
questions.explanation: The division of the cytoplasm of a eukaryotic cell into two daughter cells is known as cytokinesis. It follows the division of the cell's nucleus during mitosis and meiosis. > यूकेरियोटिक कोशिका के कोशिकाद्रव्य का दो संतति कोशिकाओं में विभाजन साइटोकाइनेसिस कहलाता है। यह माइटोसिस और मेयोसिस के दौरान कोशिका के नाभिक के विभाजन का अनुसरण करता है।

questions.question: Market structure which is dominated by only a small number of firms is called ________. > बाजार संरचना जिसमें केवल कुछ ही फर्मों का प्रभुत्व होता है उसे ________ कहा जाता है।
questions.explanation: Oligopoly is a market structure with a small number of firms, none of which can keep the others from having a significant influence. A monopoly is one firm, the duopoly is two firms and oligopoly is two or more firms. > ओलिगोपोल एक बाजार संरचना है जिसमें थोड़ी संख्या में फर्में होती हैं, जिनमें से कोई भी दूसरों को महत्वपूर्ण प्रभाव डालने से नहीं रोक सकता। एक एकाधिकार में एक फर्म होती है, डुओपली में दो फर्में होती हैं और ओलिगोपली में दो या अधिक फर्में होती हैं।

questions.question: Which of the following is a Qualitative tool used by RBI to control money supply?

questions.explanation: The marginal requirement is a qualitative tool used by the Reserve Bank of India [RBI]. It specifically targets and regulates credit distribution for certain sectors or purposes rather than impacting the overall money supply in the economy [as quantitative tools do]. > सीमा की आवश्यकता एक गुणात्मक उपकरण है जिसका प्रयोग भारतीय रिजर्व बैंक [आरबीआई] द्वारा किया जाता है। यह विशेष रूप से कुछ क्षेत्रों या उद्देश्यों के लिए ऋण वितरण को लक्षित और नियंत्रित करता है, न कि अर्थव्यवस्था में समग्र मुद्रा आपूर्ति को प्रभावित करता है [जैसा कि मात्रात्मक उपकरण करते हैं]।

questions.question: Which of the following is used for removing permanent hardness of water?
questions.explanation: Permanent hardness of water is due to dissolution of calcium and magnesium salt present in it. Permanent hardness of water is removed by adding washing soda [Sodium Carbonate] and permutit [Sodium Zeolite] method to the water. > पानी की स्थायी कठोरता उसमें उपस्थित कैल्शियम और मैग्नीशियम के लवण के घुलने के कारण होती है। पानी की स्थायी कठोरता को पानी में वॉशिंग सोडा [सोडियम कार्बोनेट] और परम्युटिट [सोडियम ज़ियोलाइट] विधि मिलाकर हटाया जाता है।

questions.question: Which one of the following non-metals shows allotropy in the liquid state?
questions.explanation: Sulphur is a non-metal that shows allotropy in the liquid state. If sulphur is heated slowly the changes between the different forms can be observed. These changes are in the form of colour and viscosity [thickness] of the liquid. > सल्फर एक अधातु है जो द्रव अवस्था में अपररूपता प्रदर्शित करती है। यदि सल्फर को धीरे-धीरे गर्म किया जाए तो इसके विभिन्न रूपों में परिवर्तन देखा जा सकता है। ये परिवर्तन तरल के रंग और श्यानता [मोटाई] के रूप में होते हैं।

questions.question: Can the government tap the phones of suspects to gather evidence ___1___ a crime is committed? What is the law on phone tapping in India, and how have High Courts interpreted it? The government's powers to intercept communication is laid down in and circumscribed by-three pieces of legislation, The Indian Post Office Act, 1898 allows for the interception of communication through post. The Indian Telegraph Act, 1885 is used for tapping voice calls; and The Information Technology Act, 2000 governs the interception of WhatsApp sages, emails, etc. The 140-year-old Telegraph ___2___ was originally meant for intercepting telegrams, but over the years it has been expanded to include telephonic conversations. Section 5(2) of the Act states that both state and central governments can, "on the occurrence of any public emergency, or ___3___ the interest of the public safety", authorize interception, Given that the right to free speech and the right to privacy are fundamental rights, any encroachment on these rights through surveillance is only ___4___ on narrow constitutional grounds. These grounds the interest of the sovereignty, and integrity of India; the security of the state; friendly relations with foreign states; public order; or preventing ___5___ to the commission of an offence-are enumerated as "reasonable restrictions" under Article 19(2) of the Constitution. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The sentence describes the government's ability to tap phones to gather evidence before a crime is committed. This implies proactive surveillance to prevent or anticipate potential crimes. The other options don't fit as well: - "After" would imply gathering evidence after the crime has been committed, which is a different context. - "Between" doesn't fit grammatically or contextually, as it implies a relationship between two points in time or events. - "Past" doesn't fit grammatically or contextually, as it would imply gathering evidence from a previous time period without specifying the relationship to the crime. The context suggests that the government is discussing its powers to intercept communication before a crime occurs, making "before" the most suitable option.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The error lies in segment: seven are forty-two. Explanation: • In mathematical expressions treated as factual statements, the subject is considered singular. • The phrase "six times seven" refers to a single multiplication fact, so the verb should also be singular. • Therefore, "are" is incorrect and should be replaced with "is." Corrected Sentence: "She answered confidently that six times seven is forty-two."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Rahul looked __________ after his team lost.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Dejected Explanation: The word "dejected" is the most appropriate choice here. It means sad or disappointed, which fits the context of someone reacting to their team’s loss. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Rejected – अस्वीकार किया गया – Means to be refused or dismissed. Example: He felt rejected after the job interview. • Dejected – उदास – Feeling downcast or disheartened. Example: She looked dejected after failing the test. • Thrilled – रोमांचित – Very excited or happy. Example: He was thrilled to win the prize. • Confused – भ्रमित – Unable to think clearly. Example: He looked confused during the instructions. Final Correct Sentence: "Rahul looked dejected after his team lost."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. He spreaded false information online and knowed it would cause trouble.
options: Spreaded false information online and knew it
Spread false information online and knew it
Spread false information online and know it
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Spread false information online and knew it Explanation: • The original sentence: "He spreaded false information online and knowed it would cause trouble" contains errors in the use of the verbs "spreaded" and "knowed." • Key issue with "spreaded": The verb "spread" is an irregular verb and does not change form in the past tense. The correct form is "spread," not "spreaded." • Key issue with "knowed": The verb "know" is also irregular, and its past tense is "knew," not "knowed." • Why option [B] is correct: "Spread false information" uses the correct past form of the verb, and "knew" correctly matches the past tense of "know." Why other options are not correct: • [A] Spreaded false information online and knew it: This is incorrect because "spreaded" is not a correct form of the verb "spread" [the correct form is "spread"]. • [C] Spread false information online and know it: This is incorrect because "know" is in the present tense, but the sentence requires the past tense "knew" to match the context. Final Correct Sentence: "He spread false information online and knew it would cause trouble."

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: Many a box of apples has been sent to the teacher by her students.
His ideas are stranger than effective.
Can I run as faster as your sister can?
Both the Girls are taller enough to clear the exam.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Many a box of apples has been sent to the teacher by her students. Explanation: Option [A] is grammatically correct. • The phrase “Many a” is always followed by a singular noun and a singular verb. • Therefore, “Many a box” correctly takes the verb “has” [not “have”]. • The sentence also follows correct passive voice structure. Why the other options are incorrect: • [B] His ideas are stranger than effective. Incorrect comparison — “stranger” [comparative of adjective] must be followed by another adjective in the same degree. Corrected: His ideas are more strange than effective. • [C] Can I run as faster as your sister can? Incorrect structure — “as fast as” is the correct comparative phrase. Corrected: Can I run as fast as your sister can? • [D] Both the Girls are taller enough to clear the exam. Incorrect placement — “enough” comes after the adjective, and “tall” is the correct form. Corrected: Both the girls are tall enough to clear the exam.

questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Stand off Explanation: The phrasal verb "stand off" is the most appropriate choice in this sentence. A "stand-off" refers to a situation where two opposing parties refuse to back down or take action, often creating tension or a deadlock. This correctly describes the tense situation between the two teams. Meanings and examples of the options: • Stand off – तनावपूर्ण स्थिति / गतिरोध – A situation in which opposing sides are unwilling to move or make concessions. Example: "The negotiation ended in a stand-off between the two companies." • Stand with – के साथ खड़े होना / समर्थन करना – To support or remain by someone’s side. Example: "I will always stand with my friends during difficult times." • Stand on – टिके रहना / किसी विश्वास पर कायम रहना – To maintain a position or belief. Example: "He continues to stand on his principles despite opposition." • Stand out – अलग दिखना / प्रमुख होना – To be noticeable or easily seen. Example: "Her creativity makes her stand out from the rest of the team." Final Correct Sentence: There was a tense stand-off between the two teams.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. She readily complied ______ my request.
questions.explanation: Correct answer: With Explanation: • The verb "comply" is always followed by the preposition "with" when referring to following a rule, instruction, or request. • "Complied with" means that someone agreed to or followed what was asked or required. • "With" connects the verb to the object [in this case, "my request"] in a grammatically correct way. Examples: • She complied with the manager’s instructions. • The company complied with all safety regulations. Corrected Sentence: She readily complied with my request.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: student and parent agree Explanation: • The phrase “Every teacher, student and parent agree” is grammatically incorrect because the subject begins with "Every", which makes it singular even if followed by a list of nouns. • Therefore, the verb should also be in the singular form — "agrees", not "agree". CONCEPT: • When "Every" is used before a list [e.g., every man, woman and child], it refers to each individual item separately. • Hence, the verb must be singular. Examples: • Every boy and girl is excited. • Every employee, manager and intern knows the rules. Corrected Sentence: Every teacher, student and parent agrees with the new rules.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph: A. The fact is, the Time Traveller was one of those men who are too clever to be believed. B. You always thought that something was hidden, that he was playing a trick on you. C. I think at that time none of us really believed in the Time Machine. D. You never felt that you knew everything about him.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: CADB Explanation: Let’s break it down step by step: • C starts with the narrator’s reflection: "I think at that time none of us really believed in the Time Machine." This sets the context. • A follows naturally by explaining why — introducing the Time Traveller and how clever he was: "The fact is, the Time Traveller was one of those men who are too clever to be believed." • D adds depth to that description, saying "You never felt that you knew everything about him." It shows he was mysterious. • B concludes the thought by explaining the result of his mysterious nature: "You always thought that something was hidden, that he was playing a trick on you." Final Paragraph: I think at that time none of us really believed in the Time Machine. The fact is, the Time Traveller was one of those men who are too clever to be believed. You never felt that you knew everything about him. You always thought that something was hidden, that he was playing a trick on you.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. Our Army has been deployed to the border.
options: The Army was deployed at the border.
The border has been deployed by our Army.
Our Army has deployed the government at the border.
The government has deployed our Army at the border.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: The government has deployed our Army at the border. Explanation: • The original sentence "Our Army has been deployed to the border" is in passive voice, where "our Army" is the object, and the doer [subject] is not mentioned. • To convert it into active voice, the object becomes the subject, and we insert a logical doer — here, "the government" is the appropriate agent. • The verb "has been deployed" [passive] becomes "has deployed" [active] in the present perfect tense. • Structure:   [Object] + has/have + past participle + by + subject Final Active Voice Sentence: The government has deployed our Army at the border.

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. The teacher said to the students, "Submit your assignments by Friday."
options: The teacher told the students submit their assignments by Friday.
The teacher told the students to submit their assignments by Friday.
The teacher said the students that submit the assignments by Friday.
The teacher ordered the students that they submit their assignments by Friday.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • The original sentence is an imperative sentence in direct speech: "Submit your assignments by Friday." • In reported speech, the reporting verb "said to" changes to "told" or "ordered", and the structure changes to include "to + base verb". • The pronoun "you" is retained as the object [the students are the ones being told]. • The word "Submit" becomes "to submit" in reported speech because the sentence is a command. • The sentence becomes a direct instruction in reported speech with "to + base verb" structure. Structure used for reported speech of imperative sentences: [Subject] + told/ordered + object + to + base verb + rest of sentence Final Sentence: The teacher told the students to submit their assignments by Friday.
